system_summary: OpenClaw is a multi-agent system designed for detecting pre-boom signals in the cryptocurrency market and managing capital asymmetrically.
The system uses a combination of liquidity monitoring, insider buying validation, and technical analysis to identify trading opportunities.
The architecture includes L-Scanner for liquidity monitoring, I-Watcher for insider buying, and T-Analyst for technical analysis.
The system operates in two phases: a simulated phase with KPIs for alpha and correlation, and a real capital phase with specific signal and risk requirements.
existing_capabilities: Simulated phase with KPIs for alpha and correlation
Real capital phase with signal and risk requirements

weaknesses.problem: The system relies heavily on external data sources for liquidity, insider buying, and technical analysis, which may not always be reliable or up-to-date.
weaknesses.evidence: The context mentions the use of snapshots and orders simulations, which may not reflect real-time market conditions.

weaknesses.problem: The system's risk management rules are not fully defined in the provided context.
weaknesses.evidence: The context mentions a risk configuration file but does not provide details on how risks are managed in real-time.

weaknesses.problem: The system's performance and scalability have not been evaluated.
weaknesses.evidence: The context does not provide information on how the system performs under high load or with a large number of assets.

overfitting_risks.evidence: The system uses historical data for simulation and may not adapt well to new market conditions.

pipeline_bottlenecks.why: The system relies on snapshots and simulated orders, which may not provide real-time data for immediate decision-making.

missing_research_lines: Evaluation of the system's performance in real-time market conditions
Detailed risk management strategies and real-time risk monitoring
Scalability analysis and optimization for handling a large number of assets
quick_wins: Implement real-time data feeds for liquidity, insider buying, and technical analysis
Develop a comprehensive risk management module with real-time monitoring
Optimize the system for scalability and performance under high load

priority_improvements.title: Real-time Data Integration
priority_improvements.problem: Reliance on snapshots and simulated orders for liquidity, insider buying, and technical analysis
priority_improvements.proposal: Integrate real-time data feeds for all market data sources

priority_improvements.title: Comprehensive Risk Management Module
priority_improvements.problem: Undefined risk management rules and lack of real-time monitoring
priority_improvements.proposal: Develop a risk management module with real-time monitoring and predefined risk thresholds

priority_improvements.title: Scalability Optimization
priority_improvements.problem: Unevaluated performance and scalability under high load
priority_improvements.proposal: Implement optimizations for handling a large number of assets and high-frequency data

experiments.name: Liquidity Monitoring with Real-time Data
experiments.hypothesis: Real-time liquidity data will improve the accuracy of liquidity monitoring
experiments.change: Replace snapshot-based liquidity data with real-time feeds
experiments.backtest_plan: Compare the performance of L-Scanner with and without real-time data over a 1-week period
experiments.automation_plan: Automate the data feed integration into L-Scanner and schedule regular updates
experiments.risk_notes: May introduce latency or data inconsistency if not properly managed

experiments.name: Real-time Insider Buying Validation
experiments.hypothesis: Real-time insider buying data will enhance the validation process
experiments.change: Incorporate real-time insider buying data into I-Watcher
experiments.backtest_plan: Assess the improvement in signal detection with real-time data compared to historical data
experiments.automation_plan: Set up a pipeline to fetch and process real-time insider buying data
success_metrics: Increase in the number of valid signals
experiments.risk_notes: Potential privacy concerns and data reliability issues

experiments.name: Technical Analysis with Real-time Data
experiments.hypothesis: Real-time technical analysis data will improve trading signals
experiments.change: Update T-Analyst to use real-time market data for technical analysis
experiments.backtest_plan: Evaluate the performance of T-Analyst with real-time data against historical data
experiments.automation_plan: Automate the integration of real-time market data into T-Analyst

architecture_changes: Incorporate real-time data processing capabilities into the existing system architecture
research_lines: Conduct a comprehensive analysis of the system's performance with real-time data feeds
Develop and test detailed risk management strategies for real-time monitoring
Perform scalability analysis and optimization for handling a large number of assets and high-frequency data

judgements.name: Liquidity Monitoring with Real-time Data
judgements.judge_note: The experiment seems well-defined with a clear hypothesis and a straightforward backtest plan. However, the risk of data inconsistency and latency needs to be managed carefully.

judgements.name: Real-time Insider Buying Validation
judgements.judge_note: The experiment is clear and has a good automation plan. However, there are potential privacy concerns and data reliability issues that need to be addressed.

judgements.name: Technical Analysis with Real-time Data
judgements.judge_note: The experiment is well-defined with a clear hypothesis and a straightforward backtest plan. However, it may require additional computational resources.
